element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Tutorial-like stages introduce core mechanics before first Robot Master encounters
Each weapon reward opens new level paths and enemy weaknesses; non-linear stage order allows player-chosen learning sequence

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Distributed across stages in Mega Man 2/3
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Items like climbing aids and temporary shields enable reaching previously inaccessible areas

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Final stage after all Robot Masters defeated
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Culmination requiring mastery of all acquired weapons and items; player must equip strategically from arsenal

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Only after completing all three remakes
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: New enemies based on Journey to the West characters; requires cross-game weapon knowledge

typical_relief_triggers: Transition between stage select screen and active play
typical_tension_triggers: Limited weapon energy forcing weapon switching
Multi-stage sequences in Wily Castle without mid-stage breaks
Genesis Unit encounters in Wily Tower (harder variants)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical tension with stage-level boundaries providing clear relief points; non-linear stage selection allows players to modulate difficulty; late-game gauntlet enforces sustained tension

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Off-path platforms and hidden item chambers discoverable through weapon experimentation or precision platforming
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Robot Master weakness sequences reward exploration and weapon testing; some stages contain secret paths unlocked only with specific acquired weapons

difficulty_curve.early_game: Mega Man 1 remake establishes baseline platforming difficulty with 6 Robot Masters offering varied stage difficulty; player choice of stage order allows customized progression curve
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Mega Man 2 and 3 remakes escalate platform complexity and enemy density; expanded weapon roster and support items create emergent difficulty through loadout decisions
difficulty_curve.late_game: Wily Castle stages remove stage select freedom, forcing gauntlet progression; Wily Tower acts as extreme difficulty cap requiring strategic weapon selection from all three games' arsenals
notable_spikes: Wily Castle transitions (no mid-stage breaks, mandatory boss gauntlet)
Genesis Unit bosses in Wily Tower (new enemies with adapted NES Mega Man AI patterns)
Final Wily confrontation (requires mastery of cross-game mechanics)

notable_level_patterns.description: Standard 2-3 screen platforming sequence with environmental hazards specific to master theme, culminating in single boss arena. Weapon drops from defeated enemies restore energy. Defeated master yields unique weapon usable in other stages.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any Robot Master stage in Mega Man 1-3 remakes

notable_level_patterns.description: Player selects which of 6-8 Robot Master stages to attempt in any order. Weapon dependencies create emergent difficulty: some stages become easier with previously acquired weapons, incentivizing multiple playthroughs or selective progression
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mega Man 1 stage select screen (6 simultaneous options)

notable_level_patterns.description: Linear, uninterrupted sequence of 4+ stages each ending in boss battle. No stage select mid-game. Removes player agency in difficulty modulation, forcing mastery of all acquired tools
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mega Man 1-3 Wily stages in remakes

notable_level_patterns.description: Three new boss encounters requiring strategic weapon selection from all three games' combined arsenals (8 weapons, 3 support items available). Exclusive unlock mechanic (all three games must be completed first) gates content as progression reward
